DECLARATION OF HANI M. SALAAM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO QUQASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT SALAAM, HANI AKA HANY SALAAM, AKA HANY M. SALAAM SPECTRUM AEROSPACE USA, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SPECTRUM AEROSPACE USA, LLC, AND, HANI SALAAM'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT SALAAM. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT HE HAD SUFFICIENT MINIMUM CONTACTS TO BE SUBJECT TO PERSONAL JURISDICTION. MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED AS TO DEFENDANT SPECTRUM AEROSPACE, AS THE CORPORATION'S ADDRESS AND BUSINESS ACTIVITIES ARE ADEQUATE TO SUBJECT IT TO GENERAL JURISDICTION UNDER KLM VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1951) 107 CAL.APP.2ND 495. COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON FORUM NON-CONVENIENS WHICH MAY BE BROUGHT AS SEPARATE MOTION. THE COURT ADDS: MOTION FOR FORUM NON-CONVENIENS IS TO BE NOTICE FOR JUNE 18, 2009, SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF IS DUE MAY 27, 2009, RESPONSE IS DUE JUNE 8, 2009, AND REPLY IS DUE JUNE 15, 2009. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANT, NO OPPOSITION FILED AND APPLICATION COMPLIES WITH CRC. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) [ERIC L. LEWIS, ESQ. OF BAACH ROBINSON & LEWIS PLLC, 1201 F STREET, NW, SUITE 500, WASHINGTON, DC 20004-1225, (202)833-8900.] (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SPECTRUM AEROSPACE USA, LLC, and, HANI SALAAM'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Evan S. Nadel, of Squire Sanders (415) 954-0392, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party;Brabara J. Chisholm, Esq. of Altshuler Berzon LLP (415) 421-7151, appearing for Defendants and moving party;Katherine Toomey, Esq. of Baach Robinson & Lewis, (202) 659-7216, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Quash is granted as to defendant Salaam. Plaintiff fails to show that he had sufficient minimum contacts to be subject to personal jurisdiction. Motion to quash is denied as to defendant Spectrum Aerospace, as the corporation's address and business activities are adequate to subject it to general jurisdiction under KLM vs. Superior Court (1951) 107 Cal.App.2nd 495. Court declines to rule on forum non-conveniens which may be brought as separate motion. The Court adds: Motion for Forum non-conveniens is to be notice for June 18, 2009, Supplemental brief is due May 27, 2009, Response is due June 8, 2009, and reply is due June 15, 2009. (Moving Party to prepare Order) (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
